items.title: AI linked to half of Australian cyber incidents, QBE
items.summary: Half of Australian businesses suffered a cyber incident last year, with QBE saying 26% involved AI and many hit by supplier-linked attacks.

items.title: The hidden operational risk behind eCommerce growth - and how to fix it
items.summary: More eCommerce sites are exposed to contractor and visitor compliance gaps as dark stores and fulfilment hubs multiply across Australia.

items.title: Liquibase launches free CVE library for Community users
items.summary: Older Liquibase Community users can now check release-by-release vulnerabilities in a free public library covering Docker images and binaries.

items.title: Check Point gains OpenAI access for defensive security
items.summary: Faster threat analysis and incident response could bolster enterprise defences as the cyber security company gets access to GPT-5.5.

items.title: ConnectWise launches AI-native platform for Predictive IT
items.summary: MSPs could trim ticket loads and lift margins as the new platform ties AI automation to service workflows and security operations.

items.title: PureVPN launches ChatGPT tool for VPN server access
items.summary: Users in 10 markets can now find and connect to PureVPN servers through ChatGPT prompts, without sharing data with the chatbot.

items.title: Texas retailers hit harder by organised crime, Auror says
items.summary: A small group of repeat offenders is driving most retail crime in Texas, with violence and weapons featured in one in eight incidents.

items.title: Chatbots Keep Telling Stories About Lighthouse Keeper 'Elias Thorne'. We Might Know Why
items.summary: LLMs including ChatGPT, Gemini and Claude are obsessed with telling stories about lighthouse keepers and clockmakers, and one character named 'Elias Thorne' has made his way from chatbots to Amazon books. Researchers are trying to discover why.

items.title: Cops Keep Getting Arrested for Using Flock to Stalk People
items.summary: There have been more than a dozen cases around the country where police use Flock to obsessively and illegally stalk people.

items.summary: Microsoft took the highly unusual step of shutting down more than 70 of its own GitHub repositories after hackers pushed malware that would steal credentials from AI coding agent users.

items.title: 1,000 Data Breaches Later, the Disclosure Lag is Worse Than Ever
items.summary: Today, I loaded the 1,000th data breach into Have I Been Pwned. Reflecting on that milestone number, I pondered how to mark the occasion in writing, and what immediately came to mind was a very simple question: why is it still needed? Especially considering the emergence of privacy regulations
